title: Volvulus interior

This genus consists of plants that are typically annuals or perennials with small, daisy-like flowers. The classification of Volvulus interior places it within the broader group of composites, which includes many types of flowering plants. Further research may be needed to determine the exact relationships within the family Asteraceae.

intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant 'Volvulus interior'. However, I found information on the plant 'Verbascum thapsus' (also known as 'Volvulus thapsus' or 'Barbed Mullein') and 'Verbascum sinuatum' (also known as 'Silverbird' or 'Verbascum interior'). 

Here is a paragraph about 'Verbascum thapsus':

Verbascum thapsus, commonly known as Barbed Mullein, is a biennial plant in the Scrophulariaceae family, characterized by its tall, upright growth and clusters of yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Verbascum thapsus include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Verbascum thapsus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to relieve coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
